description: Personal single-user news reader that aggregates RSS feeds through an AI editorial layer

environment.notes: Server-rendered HTML via Hono, no SPA framework. SQLite database via Drizzle ORM.

userStories.title: Project scaffolding
userStories.description: Initialise Bun + TypeScript project with Hono server, Drizzle ORM, and Vitest
acceptanceCriteria: Running `bun run dev` starts an Hono HTTP server on a configurable port
GET / returns HTTP 200
TypeScript strict mode is enabled in tsconfig.json
`bun run test` executes Vitest and passes with at least one smoke test
`bun run typecheck` runs tsc --noEmit with zero errors

userStories.title: Database schema and migrations
userStories.description: Define Drizzle ORM schema for raw_content, articles, editions, and bookmarks tables and apply migrations
acceptanceCriteria: Drizzle schema defines raw_content table with columns: id, source_name, source_url, title, body_text, published_at, content_hash, processed, ingested_at
Drizzle schema defines articles table with columns: id, raw_content_id, headline, summary, body, category, importance_score, source_name, source_url, published_at, is_brief, created_at
Drizzle schema defines editions table with columns: id, edition_date (unique), brief_article_ids (JSON text), generated_at
Drizzle schema defines bookmarks table with columns: id, article_id, bookmarked_at
Database migrations run successfully and all four tables are queryable

userStories.title: Application configuration
userStories.description: Centralised config for feed URLs, categories, and editorial settings loaded from a config file
acceptanceCriteria: A config module exports a typed configuration object with feedUrls (array of {name, url}), categories (array of strings defaulting to ['AI & Tech', 'Business', 'Science', 'General']), and editorial settings
Config values can be overridden via a JSON config file at a known path
Importing the config module in tests returns valid defaults without requiring the config file to exist
Adding or removing a feed URL in the config file is reflected when the config is reloaded

userStories.title: RSS/Atom feed parser
userStories.description: Parse RSS 2.0 and Atom XML feeds into a normalised array of feed entries
acceptanceCriteria: Given valid RSS 2.0 XML, the parser returns an array of entries each with title, body_text, published_at (Date), and link
Given valid Atom XML, the parser returns the same normalised entry shape
Malformed XML returns an empty array without throwing
Entries missing a title or link are excluded from the result

userStories.title: RSS ingestion pipeline with trigger endpoint
userStories.description: Fetch configured RSS feeds, deduplicate by content hash, store new entries in raw_content, and expose a manual trigger endpoint
acceptanceCriteria: POST /api/ingest/trigger fetches all configured feed URLs and returns HTTP 200 with a JSON body containing {ingested: number, skipped: number}
New entries are inserted into raw_content with processed=false and a SHA-256 content_hash of title+body_text
Duplicate entries (same content_hash already in raw_content) are skipped and counted in the skipped field
source_name and source_url on each raw_content row match the feed config entry
If a feed URL is unreachable, ingestion continues for remaining feeds and the response includes an errors array

userStories.title: Editorial processing engine
userStories.description: Process unprocessed raw_content through Claude API to produce structured editorial output with daily brief and categorised feed items
acceptanceCriteria: The editorial engine queries all raw_content rows where processed=false and sends them to the Claude API with a system prompt
Claude's response is parsed as JSON containing daily_brief (array of 5-6 items) and categorised_feed (remaining items), each with headline, summary, category, and importance_score
Each editorial output item is written to the articles table with is_brief=true for daily_brief items and is_brief=false for categorised_feed items
An editions row is created with today's date, the brief article IDs as a JSON array, and generated_at timestamp
All processed raw_content rows are marked processed=true
userStories.notes: Claude API calls should be abstracted behind an interface for test mocking

userStories.title: Editorial trigger endpoint
userStories.description: Expose an API endpoint to manually trigger the editorial processing pipeline
acceptanceCriteria: POST /api/editorial/trigger runs the editorial engine and returns HTTP 200 with {briefCount: number, feedCount: number, editionDate: string}
If no unprocessed raw_content exists, the endpoint returns HTTP 200 with {briefCount: 0, feedCount: 0, editionDate: null}
Brief article summaries are between 80 and 120 words; categorised feed summaries are between 40 and 60 words

userStories.title: Articles API
userStories.description: API endpoints for listing articles with pagination and category filtering, and retrieving a single article by ID
acceptanceCriteria: GET /api/articles returns a JSON array of articles sorted by importance_score descending, limited to 20 by default
GET /api/articles?category=AI%20%26%20Tech returns only articles matching the given category
GET /api/articles?limit=5&offset=10 returns 5 articles starting from offset 10
GET /api/articles/:id returns the full article object with all fields or HTTP 404 if not found

userStories.title: Today's brief API
userStories.description: API endpoint returning today's edition with its brief articles
acceptanceCriteria: GET /api/brief/today returns a JSON object with edition_date, generated_at, and an articles array containing the brief articles for today's edition
The articles array contains full article objects in the order specified by brief_article_ids
If no edition exists for today, the endpoint returns HTTP 200 with {edition_date: null, articles: []}

userStories.title: Bookmarks API
userStories.description: CRUD endpoints for managing article bookmarks
acceptanceCriteria: POST /api/bookmarks with {articleId: number} creates a bookmark and returns HTTP 201 with the bookmark object
POST /api/bookmarks with a non-existent articleId returns HTTP 404
DELETE /api/bookmarks/:articleId removes the bookmark and returns HTTP 200
GET /api/bookmarks returns a JSON array of bookmarked articles ordered by bookmarked_at descending

userStories.title: Today page UI
userStories.description: Server-rendered landing page at / showing daily brief cards and categorised feed
acceptanceCriteria: GET / returns HTML containing a 'Daily Brief' section with horizontally-scrollable cards, each showing headline, source name, and elapsed time
Each brief card links to /article/:id
Below the brief, a 'Feed' section groups articles by category with category headers, each article showing headline, summary (truncated to 2 lines), source, and elapsed time
Feed articles within each category are sorted by importance_score descending
The page uses serif fonts for headlines/body and sans-serif for UI elements, with #E3120B as the accent colour

userStories.title: Article detail page
userStories.description: Server-rendered article page at /article/:id showing full content with navigation
acceptanceCriteria: GET /article/:id returns HTML displaying headline, source name, published date, and full body text
The page includes a back link to /
If the article is a brief item (is_brief=true), prev/next links navigate to adjacent brief articles in the current edition
GET /article/999999 (non-existent) returns HTTP 404

userStories.title: Category feed page
userStories.description: Server-rendered page at /category/:slug showing articles filtered to one category for the last 7 days
acceptanceCriteria: GET /category/ai-tech returns HTML listing only articles with category 'AI & Tech' from the last 7 days
Articles are sorted by importance_score descending
Each article row shows headline, summary, source, and elapsed time
GET /category/nonexistent returns HTML with an empty state message
userStories.notes: Slug-to-category mapping: ai-tech → 'AI & Tech', business → 'Business', science → 'Science', general → 'General'

userStories.title: Saved articles page
userStories.description: Server-rendered page at /saved showing bookmarked articles
acceptanceCriteria: GET /saved returns HTML listing bookmarked articles ordered by bookmarked_at descending
Each item shows headline, source, elapsed time, and a remove-bookmark action
When no bookmarks exist, the page shows an empty state message
Clicking remove-bookmark removes the bookmark and refreshes the page

userStories.title: Bookmark toggle on article detail
userStories.description: Client-side bookmark button on the article detail page that toggles bookmark state via the API
acceptanceCriteria: The article detail page shows a bookmark button that displays 'Bookmark' when not bookmarked and 'Bookmarked' when bookmarked
Clicking the button when not bookmarked sends POST /api/bookmarks and updates the button state without a full page reload
Clicking the button when bookmarked sends DELETE /api/bookmarks/:articleId and updates the button state without a full page reload
userStories.notes: Minimal client-side JS using fetch API

userStories.title: Seed script with mock data
userStories.description: A seed script that populates the database with 20 mock articles across all categories and one mock edition
acceptanceCriteria: Running the seed script inserts exactly 20 articles distributed across all configured categories
5-6 of the seeded articles have is_brief=true
One editions row is created with today's date referencing the brief article IDs
Running the seed script twice does not create duplicate articles (idempotent)
